description: Looks up the reputation of the indicator in the Enterprise collection only.
examples:
Command Example
#
!attack-pattern attack_pattern="Search Threat Vendor Data"
Human Readable Output
#
MITRE ATTACK
#
Name: Search Threat Vendor Data - ID: T1681

!attack-pattern attack_pattern="Search Threat Vendor Data"
Human Readable Output
#
MITRE ATTACK
#
Name: Search Threat Vendor Data - ID: T1681
#
Threat actors may seek information/indicators from closed or open threat intelligence sources gathered about their own campaigns, as well as those conducted by other adversaries that may align with their target industries, capabilities/objectives, or other operational concerns. These reports may include descriptions of behavior, detailed breakdowns of attacks, atomic indicators such as malware hashes or IP addresses, timelines of a group’s activity, and more. Adversaries may change their behavior when planning their future operations.

outputs.description: The indicator that was tested. DBotScore.Score The actual score.
outputs.name: DBotScore.Indicator

outputs.description: The indicator type. DBotScore.Vendor The vendor used to calculate the score.
outputs.name: DBotScore.Type

outputs.description: The STIX ID of the Attack Pattern. AttackPattern.KillChainPhases The kill chain phases of the Attack Pattern.
outputs.name: AttackPattern.STIXID

outputs.description: The first seen by source of the Attack Pattern. AttackPattern.Description The description of the Attack Pattern.
outputs.name: AttackPattern.FirstSeenBySource

outputs.description: The operating system references of the Attack Pattern. AttackPattern.Publications The publications of the Attack Pattern.
outputs.name: AttackPattern.OperatingSystemRefs

outputs.description: The MITRE ID of the Attack Pattern. AttackPattern.Tags The tags of the Attack Pattern.
outputs.name: AttackPattern.MITREID
